steps.title: Find angle C in triangle ABC using angle sum
steps.content:
In triangle ABC, the angles sum to 180°. With angle A = 69° and angle B = 90°:
$$69° + 90° + C = 180°$$
$$C = 21°$$

steps.title: Use congruence to determine angle F
steps.content: Triangles ABC and DEF are congruent with A corresponding to D. Since both B and E are right angles, B corresponds to E. Therefore, C corresponds to F. In congruent triangles, corresponding angles are equal, so angle F = C = 21°.
